input_text: How long will I have irregular periods after a miscarriage?
gold_action: Insufficient grounding evidence to state a specific action; recommend general clinical consultation.
raw_response:
{
  "predicted_category": "menstrual",
  "interpreted_symptom": "Irregular periods following a miscarriage",
  "predicted_risk": "routine",
  "recommended_action": "Explain that irregular periods are common after a miscarriage as the body and hormones recover, typically returning to normal within 1-3 cycles, but advise seeing a doctor if irregularity persists beyond 3 months or if there are other concerning symptoms.",
  "asks_clarification": false,
  "clarifying_question": "",
  "unsafe_response": false,
  "response_text": "It is very common to have irregular periods for a few months after a miscarriage as your body and hormone levels return to their pre-pregnancy state. Most women's cycles normalize within 1 to 3 months. However, if your periods remain irregular for longer than 3 months, or if you experience severe pain, heavy bleeding, or other concerning symptoms, please consult your healthcare provider to rule out other causes and ensure your recovery is on track."
}
recommended_action: Explain that irregular periods are common after a miscarriage as the body and hormones recover, typically returning to normal within 1-3 cycles, but advise seeing a doctor if irregularity persists beyond 3 months or if there are other concerning symptoms.
response_text: It is very common to have irregular periods for a few months after a miscarriage as your body and hormone levels return to their pre-pregnancy state. Most women's cycles normalize within 1 to 3 months. However, if your periods remain irregular for longer than 3 months, or if you experience severe pain, heavy bleeding, or other concerning symptoms, please consult your healthcare provider to rule out other causes and ensure your recovery is on track.

input_text: After I lost the pregnancy, my monthly cycle hasn't gone back to normal — how long does that usually take?
gold_action: Insufficient grounding evidence to state a specific action; recommend general clinical consultation.
raw_response:
{
  "predicted_category": "menstrual",
  "interpreted_symptom": "Irregular or absent menstrual cycles following a pregnancy loss",
  "predicted_risk": "routine",
  "recommended_action": "Consult a healthcare provider if cycles do not return to normal within 3 months post-loss.",
  "asks_clarification": false,
  "clarifying_question": "",
  "unsafe_response": false,
  "response_text": "It is common for menstrual cycles to take some time to regulate after a pregnancy loss. For most people, periods return within 4 to 6 weeks, though it can take up to 3 months for the cycle to fully normalize. If you haven't had a period within 3 months, or if you experience severe pain, heavy bleeding, or other concerning symptoms, please consult your healthcare provider to rule out underlying issues."
}
interpreted_symptom: Irregular or absent menstrual cycles following a pregnancy loss
recommended_action: Consult a healthcare provider if cycles do not return to normal within 3 months post-loss.
response_text: It is common for menstrual cycles to take some time to regulate after a pregnancy loss. For most people, periods return within 4 to 6 weeks, though it can take up to 3 months for the cycle to fully normalize.
